hypothesis: A force-uncertainty-weighted BAX acquisition that selects points with maximal predicted per-atom force variance will reduce the number of expensive energy/force simulator evaluations required for NN-BAX to meet the same NEB convergence criteria by at least 30% compared to the random-path sampling acquisition used in this paper for both the LJ38 benchmark and DFT-calculated systems of ≥100 atoms, while maintaining predicted energy-barrier errors within 2% of ground truth.
